rag_rules: When fixing AGREE diagnostics, ensure that all identifiers resolve to visible features or declared helpers in the current scope.
For implementation-level contracts, use assign only for valid output features or declared auxiliary variables; avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope.
If AGREE reports type mismatches or unresolved references, trace the root cause and either fix the reference or remove the invalid clause.
generic_rules: AGREE expressions must maintain type consistency; numeric, boolean, and package-qualified references must align with available model units.
Component type annexes should only reference features visible at that scope; implementation-level bindings belong in implementation annexes.
Generated AGREE code should not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed model.
When resolving diagnostics, prefer removing invalid clauses over adding new architecture elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks with valid syntax: assume, guarantee, eq, const, assign.
Helper functions and variables must be declared within the same annex and match the types of their expressions.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions; avoid referencing undefined or inaccessible identifiers.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new package-qualified identifiers or data types that are not part of the current model's dependencies.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes; move them to implementation scope or remove them.
Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by inventing new architecture; instead, rewrite or delete clauses using only visible identifiers.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about unreachable or inadequate port types
AGREE validator diagnostics about annex placement or assume/guarantee syntax
Model fusion or owner/scope preservation rules
